_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to develop and modify questions that are suitable for investigating contemporary economic, business, work, or financial issues and systems.

_source.w: We are learning to develop and modify questions that are suitable for investigating contemporary economic, business, work, or financial issues and systems.
y: The ability to formulate and refine investigation questions is the foundation of rigorous economic inquiry.
Modifying questions based on emerging evidence demonstrates intellectual flexibility and depth.
This metacognitive skill — thinking about how we think — transfers to every area of study and work.
eg: Developing a research question about a current economic issue and refining it as our understanding deepens.
Modifying our initial question when early research reveals that the issue is more complex than expected.
Creating questions at different levels of complexity — descriptive, analytical, evaluative.
Peer-reviewing and improving each other's investigation questions.
Comparing our questions with those used in published economic research.
Reflecting on how our questions evolved throughout the investigation and what this reveals about our learning.
